chunk_summary: This section provides a stepwise approach to calculate energy and protein requirements for Indian children with chronic undernutrition, using ICMR-NIN guidelines and worked examples for practical application.
learning_objectives: Understand the stepwise method for calculating energy and protein requirements in children with chronic undernutrition.
Learn how to adjust nutritional targets for stunted children based on weight-for-age and wasting status.
Apply ICMR-NIN reference values to determine baseline energy and protein needs for different age groups.
Develop practical strategies for implementing catch-up nutrition in stunted children.
key_facts: Baseline energy needs: 83 kcal/kg/day (1–3 years), 74 kcal/kg/day (4–6 years), 67 kcal/kg/day (7–9 years).
Baseline protein needs: 0.97 g/kg/day (1–3 years), 0.87 g/kg/day (4–6 years), 0.92 g/kg/day (7–9 years).
For stunting with preserved weight-for-height, increase energy by 10–15% above baseline.
For stunting with low weight-for-age or mild wasting, increase energy by 20–30% above baseline.
Example: A 2-year-old weighing 10 kg requires 830 kcal/day and 9.7 g/day protein; with a 15% increment, the target becomes 955 kcal/day.
reference_quote: A practical clinic shortcut from ICMR-NIN reference values is energy about 83 kcal/kg/day at 1–3 years, 74 kcal/kg/day at 4–6 years, and 67 kcal/kg/day at 7–9 years.
document_key_topics: Stunting and chronic undernutrition in children
